title: Enhanced Eagle Logo Entry Animation with Neon Glow, Service Button Navigation, and Global Smoothness

requirements.summary: Enhance eagle logo entry animation so neon glow activates from head first with strongest electric blue glow on head and eyes visible from first frame
acceptanceCriteria: Eagle head has strongest electric blue neon glow visible from first animation frame
Head area has three glow layers: inner illumination, medium outline glow, soft outer ambient aura
Head and eyes appear as the energy source with brightest glow intensity
Glow exists before animation begins (not animated from zero opacity)

file_operations.description: Enhance the eagle logo SVG structure to add layered neon glow effects on the head and eyes. Apply three distinct glow layers: innerGlow (strongest, tight to head/eyes), mediumGlow (outline around head), and ambientGlow (soft outer aura). Set initial glow opacity values so glow is visible from first render (not animated from zero). Use electric blue color (#00f7ff) with higher intensity on head/eyes region. Ensure glow exists before any animation begins by setting CSS filter drop-shadow or SVG filter effects with non-zero opacity in the initial state.

requirements.summary: Create energy spread effect where glow flows smoothly from head to wings to full body with light propagation effect lasting 0.4-0.6s
acceptanceCriteria: Glow propagates from head to wings to body smoothly over 0.4–0.6 seconds
Light bloom burst appears at eagle head at animation start
Optional shimmer sweep effect moves across wings once
No flashing or harsh transitions during energy spread
Animation feels like AI emblem powering on from brain/head

file_operations.description: Add CSS keyframe animations for energy spread effect during entry animation. Create @keyframes for: (1) glowSpread animation that increases glow intensity/radius from head→wings→body over 0.4-0.6s with smooth easing, (2) headBloomBurst animation for short light bloom burst at head on animation start, (3) optional shimmerSweep animation that moves gradient sweep across wings once. Apply animations using animation property with staggered delays to create propagation effect. Use opacity and filter adjustments (brightness, blur) to create light flow without harsh transitions. Trigger animations when hasAnimated state transitions or via animation class toggle.

Add global @keyframes definitions for eagle logo energy spread animations: glowSpread, headBloomBurst, and shimmerSweep. Define smooth timing functions (ease-out or cubic-bezier) for natural light propagation feel. Include GPU-accelerated properties where possible (transform, opacity) to ensure smooth performance.

requirements.summary: Animate eagle logo parent container with opacity, scale, and translateY transforms over 1-1.2s ease-out, then stabilize glow into continuous breathing pulse with head brighter
acceptanceCriteria: Parent container animates with opacity, scale, and translateY transforms over 1–1.2 seconds
Easing function is ease-out
Only GPU-accelerated transforms are used
After entry completes, glow transitions to soft breathing pulse
Eagle head maintains higher brightness than wings and body in final state

file_operations.description: Modify the parent container (wrapper div) animation to use transform properties: opacity: 0→1, scale: 0.85→1, translateY: 25px→0 over duration 1-1.2s with ease-out timing. Apply transform using CSS transitions or keyframe animation on the container element. After entry animation completes, add continuous breathing pulse animation using @keyframes that subtly adjusts glow filter brightness/opacity in a loop (e.g., 3-4s duration, alternate direction). Ensure head region retains higher brightness multiplier than wings/body in final state by applying stronger filter or additional glow layer to head elements.

Add @keyframes breathingPulse animation for continuous soft glow pulsing effect. Use subtle filter brightness or opacity changes (e.g., 1.0 to 1.15 and back) with ease-in-out timing. Ensure animation is smooth and non-distracting. Define utility classes or animation names that can be applied to eagle logo after entry completes.

requirements.summary: Link all service detail 'Get Started' buttons to smoothly scroll to Get In Touch section with button press animation and sticky header offset
acceptanceCriteria: All service detail 'Get Started' buttons trigger smooth scroll to Get In Touch section
Button click includes small press animation before scroll begins
Scrolling is animated and smooth, not instant
Sticky header offset is accounted for so Get In Touch section is fully visible
No page reload or navigation to another page occurs
Behavior is consistent on mobile and desktop

file_operations.description: Modify the 'Get Started' button onClick handler to: (1) Apply a small press animation (scale down slightly, e.g., scale: 0.95) using CSS transition or inline style change, (2) After brief delay (e.g., 150ms), call scrollToSection utility function targeting 'contact' section ID. Use the existing scrollToSection helper from frontend/src/utils/scrollHelpers.ts which already handles smooth scrolling with offset compensation for sticky header. Ensure button does not trigger page reload or navigation. Test behavior on both mobile and desktop viewports.

Review and ensure scrollToSection function properly accounts for sticky navbar height when calculating scroll offset. Verify smooth scroll behavior is enabled and offset calculation matches current navbar height. If needed, adjust offset calculation to ensure target section is fully visible below sticky header on all screen sizes.

requirements.summary: Add highlight effect to Get In Touch section when scrolled to from service buttons: soft fade-in and subtle glow activation around contact form
acceptanceCriteria: Get In Touch section has soft fade-in effect when scrolled to from service buttons
Contact form area receives subtle glow activation to highlight it
Highlight effect is noticeable but not jarring
Effect triggers consistently when navigating from any service detail

file_operations.description: Add event listener for custom highlight event (e.g., 'section-highlight' from scrollHelpers). When event fires, apply CSS class that triggers: (1) soft fade-in animation on section container, (2) subtle glow effect (box-shadow or filter) around contact cards/form area. Use CSS transition or keyframe animation with 0.5-0.8s duration. Remove highlight class after animation completes to allow re-triggering. Ensure effect is noticeable but not jarring using gentle easing and moderate glow intensity.

Review scrollToSectionWithHighlight function to ensure it dispatches custom event to target section after scroll completes. Verify event payload includes section ID so ContactSection can respond appropriately. Ensure timing allows scroll to complete before highlight triggers.

Add CSS classes for section highlight effect: .section-highlight with fade-in animation and .contact-highlight-glow with subtle box-shadow or filter glow. Define @keyframes for fade-in and glow-activate animations. Use electric blue or neon cyan colors matching brand theme with low opacity for subtlety.

requirements.summary: Enable smooth scroll behavior globally and add 0.6s ease transitions to interactive elements with cubic-bezier easing for buttons
acceptanceCriteria: Global smooth scroll behavior is enabled site-wide
Interactive elements have 0.6s ease transitions applied globally
All buttons use cubic-bezier easing for press/hover interactions
Scroll behavior feels smooth without lag

file_operations.description: Add global CSS rules: (1) Set html { scroll-behavior: smooth; } for site-wide smooth scrolling, (2) Add global transition rule for interactive elements: a, button, input, [role='button'] { transition: all 0.6s ease; }, (3) Define specific button transition with cubic-bezier easing: button, .btn { transition: transform 0.6s cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1), background-color 0.6s ease, box-shadow 0.6s ease; }. Ensure transitions are smooth without causing layout shift or jank.

requirements.summary: Add fade and translateY animations to sections on scroll, reduce animation jumps, enable GPU acceleration for all animations
acceptanceCriteria: Sections animate with fade-in and translateY when scrolling into view
Animation timing is smooth without sudden jumps or jank
GPU acceleration (transform3d, will-change) is enabled for animations
Overall website scroll experience feels fluid and premium

file_operations.description: Review and enhance the useSectionAnimation hook to ensure smooth fade-in and translateY animations. Adjust animation timing, easing, and intersection observer thresholds to reduce sudden jumps. Consider adding staggered delays for child elements if applicable. Ensure hook returns animation state that applies GPU-accelerated CSS classes.

Add GPU acceleration hints to all animated elements: will-change: transform, opacity; and use transform: translate3d() instead of translateY for hardware acceleration. Review existing animation keyframes (fade-in, slide-up, etc.) and ensure they use transform3d for translateY effects. Add utility classes for GPU-accelerated animations: .gpu-accelerated { will-change: transform, opacity; transform: translate3d(0, 0, 0); }. Apply backface-visibility: hidden and perspective hints where needed to improve animation smoothness.

Review section rendering and ensure all sections use the useSectionAnimation hook consistently. Verify that animation classes are applied correctly and that there are no conflicting animations causing jumps. Adjust animation delays or stagger timings if needed to create smooth sequential reveals as user scrolls.
